steps.prompt: Which word functions as the verb/participle?

hints.to speak well: While 'benedicere' literally means 'to speak well of', the standard gloss is 'to bless'

hints.present indicative: 'Benedicta' is not a finite verb form; it is a participle used as predicate adjective

hints.dico, dicere, dixi, dictum: These are the principal parts of 'dico' (to say); 'benedico' is a compound of this
hints.bene, —, —, —: 'Bene' is an adverb, not a verb; it does not have principal parts
hints.laudo, laudare, laudavi, laudatum: These are the principal parts of 'laudo' (to praise)

hints.vocative: While 'tu' can be vocative, here it serves as the subject of the predicate 'benedicta'

hints.nominative: Nominative plural would also be 'mulieres', but after 'inter' (a preposition taking accusative) it is accusative
hints.ablative: Ablative plural would be 'mulieribus'; 'inter' takes the accusative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns end in -a; 'mulier' has genitive 'mulieris'
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um; 'mulier' has genitive 'mulieris'
hints.5th declension: 5th declension nouns end in -es with genitive -ei; 'mulier' has genitive 'mulieris'
